items.text: A production ActiveWorkflowRecoverySeam is implemented that recovers active workflows by replaying their event history through the durability executor, restoring process state including workflow version, run ID, and process PID.
aion-server's engine builder installs the production recovery seam so that server restart with in-flight workflows succeeds.
An integration test demonstrates crash-resume: start workflow, stop engine, create new engine with same store, verify workflow resumes from last recorded state.
arm_timer and disarm_timer in the timer NIF bridge delegate to the engine's actual timer wheel, replacing the current no-ops.
TimerRecovery is constructed and run during engine startup, scanning expired timers from the store and delivering them to their workflows.
sleep is implemented as a durable timer (record TimerStarted, arm in wheel, await TimerFired) rather than an in-memory tokio::time::sleep.
The with_timeout NIF decodes the operation argument as a callable (Gleam function reference), matching the aion_flow SDK's with_timeout(operation: fn() -> Result(a, e), timeout_ms: Int) signature.
The NIF executes the operation closure within a deadline race and returns either the operation's result or a timeout error.
The with_timeout outcome is recorded as a durability event so replay returns the recorded result without re-executing.
handle_subscription_socket is mounted on the documented API path in the HTTP router alongside existing REST routes.
The mounted route uses the existing WebSocketConfig for connection parameters.
A test client can upgrade to WebSocket on the route and receive streamed events.
aion_flow exports continue_as_new(input: a) -> Result(Never, WorkflowError) that serializes the input and calls the backing NIF.
The continue_as_new NIF records a ContinueAsNew event through the Recorder and signals the process to terminate with the continuation input.
The engine handles ContinueAsNew termination by starting a new run of the same workflow type with the provided input and fresh event history.
